prerequisites: A Snowflake account with an accessible account hostname (the value for the host field, typically <account_identifier>.snowflakecomputing.com).
A Snowflake user (username) that can authenticate and a role granted to that user.
A warehouse the role can use to execute the load.
A target database and schema that exist in the account.
Privileges on the role to write data: USAGE on the warehouse, USAGE on the database, and USAGE plus CREATE on the schema so destination tables can be created and written.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with the Snowflake username plus a password (credentials.password). The connecting user must have the supplied role, warehouse, database, and schema.

authMethods.name: Key pair authentication
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an RSA key pair instead of a password. Provide the PEM private key (credentials.private_key), and if the key is encrypted, its passphrase (credentials.private_key_password). The corresponding public key must be assigned to the user with ALTER USER <user> SET RSA_PUBLIC_KEY='...'. Snowflake requires a minimum 2048-bit RSA key.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify the Snowflake user and role
setupSteps.body: In Snowflake, identify the user (username) that will run the load and the role it will use. Roles are the entities that hold privileges; grant the role to the user and confirm it can be activated in a session.

setupSteps.title: Grant write privileges to the role
setupSteps.body: Grant the role USAGE on the target warehouse, USAGE on the database, and USAGE plus CREATE on the schema so it can create and write to destination tables. Privileges are assigned with GRANT <privilege> ... TO ROLE <role>.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: For password auth, set the user's password and supply it in credentials.password. For key pair auth, generate a 2048-bit (or larger) RSA key pair with OpenSSL (openssl genrsa 2048 | openssl pkcs8 -topk8 ... -out rsa_key.p8, then openssl rsa -in rsa_key.p8 -pubout -out rsa_key.pub), assign the public key to the user via ALTER USER <user> SET RSA_PUBLIC_KEY='<key without delimiters>', and supply the private key in credentials.private_key. If the private key is encrypted with a passphrase, supply it in credentials.private_key_password.

setupSteps.title: Fill in the connection configuration
setupSteps.body: Set host to your Snowflake account hostname, and provide the required username, role, warehouse, database, and schema. Optionally set raw_data_schema, jdbc_url_params, retention_period_days, cdc_deletion_mode, disable_type_dedupe, and trim_space as needed.
